summary: Shaw shares the completion of a months-long cast drawing rendered in pencil on claybord, which he named 'Saint Anger' after the Metallica album. He explains that he names his cast drawings after metal albums because listening to metal keeps him sane during the meticulous rendering process. He outlines his next steps in classical art training, including Bargue plates and master copies, but declares he's done with pencil work.
key_points: Completed a detailed cast drawing that took months of meticulous pencil rendering on claybord
Uses metal album names for cast drawings as a personal coping mechanism for tedious work
Plans to progress through classical art training: Bargue plates, cast paintings, and master copies
Expresses frustration with pencil as a medium despite completing a disciplined piece
money_quotes: I name my cast drawings after metal albums because without metal I would go insane doing this meticulous rendering.
But fuck this pencil shit. Life's too short.
key_insight: Shaw documents his serious commitment to classical art training while maintaining his characteristic irreverence about the process.
